# Create complete previews and reconcile them from recorded state

A new or recreated Preview Environment deploys its full topology. Later Preview Plans run after the per-preview concurrency lock and reconcile each Deployment Unit from Preview State recorded as successful GitHub Deployments: they compare the unit's exact last successfully delivered pull-request commit with the current head, select units affected through workspace or delivery dependencies, repair missing expected stacks, and expand the selection to delivery dependants. Missing, invalid, or failed state is never treated as current, and unrelated history after a force-push or rebase triggers complete reconciliation.

Playwright uses separate Preview Validation State so runtime validation can run without mutating an already-current environment and a later non-runtime commit cannot hide an earlier failure. Existing API configuration is read from the Preview Environment when Accounts API does not require deployment; planning failures fail closed, and successful Deployment Units remain current when Playwright alone fails.
